data.title: [Sprint/Phase7]: epic closed before dso:completion-verifier dispatched on epic-level SCs
data.description:
## Expected Behavior

Phase 7 Step 0.75 of the sprint SKILL.md requires dispatching dso:completion-verifier to verify all epic-level success criteria BEFORE calling ticket transition to close the epic. The verifier is the authoritative gate; the orchestrator is explicitly prohibited from substituting inline verification (CLAUDE.md rule 24: 'Inline verification is NOT a substitute — the verifier exists because the orchestrator is biased toward confirming its own work').

## Actual Behavior

In sprint session 2026-04-09 (epic 6cd2-da08, 'Proactive self-improvement: agents suggest guidance improvements during normal work'), the orchestrator:

1. Ran completion-verifier on 6 stories (as a general-purpose agent with inline content — see Bug 2 for why named agent dispatch failed)
2. Called `.claude/scripts/dso ticket transition 6cd2-da08 in_progress closed` — closing the epic
3. ONLY THEN ran dso:completion-verifier on the epic-level SCs, after the user pointed out the omission

The epic-level verifier returned PASS on all 6 SCs, so no coverage gap was introduced this time. However, the ordering violation means a FAIL result from the epic verifier would have been discovered AFTER the epic was already closed, making it structurally impossible for the gate to block a premature closure.

## Root Cause

The orchestrator confused 'run verifier per story at Step 10a' with 'run verifier on epic at Phase 7 Step 0.75'. When transitioning from story closure (Step 10a) to epic closure (Phase 7), the epic-level verifier dispatch step was skipped. The orchestrator moved directly from 'all stories closed' to 'close epic' without the intermediate Phase 7 Step 0.75 verifier dispatch.

Contributing factor: the verifier was already dispatched for stories, creating a false sense that verification was complete. The epic-level verifier serves a distinct purpose (verifies SC1-SC6 coherently against the full implementation, including cross-story integration) and cannot be substituted by per-story verifiers.

## Evidence

- Session: worktree-20260409-084011, epic 6cd2-da08
- Epic was transitioned to closed at approximately 2026-04-09T16:30Z
- Epic-level dso:completion-verifier was dispatched approximately 5 minutes AFTER the closed transition, only after user escalation
- CLAUDE.md rule 24: 'the orchestrator MUST dispatch the verifier sub-agent at story closure (Step 10a) + epic closure (Phase 7 Step 0.75)'

## Fix Directions

1. Add an explicit pre-condition check in Phase 7: before any epic ticket transition, confirm completion-verifier has been dispatched and returned PASS for the epic
2. Add an assertion comment in the sprint SKILL.md Phase 7 header: 'GATE: must have dispatched completion-verifier on the epic at Step 0.75 before proceeding to step 1'
3. Consider making the epic transition gate enforce this via a ticket comment sentinel (similar to how review-gate uses a review-status file)
